contraventionDescription: Failing to comply with a sign indicating restrictions on vehicles entering a pedestrian zone, and, failing to comply with a sign indicating restrictions on vehicles entering and waiting in a pedestrian zone. Contravention code 53

sections.heading: Failing to comply with a sign indicating restrictions on vehicles entering a pedestrian zone, and, failing to comply with a sign indicating restrictions on vehicles entering and waiting in a pedestrian zone. Contravention code 53
sections.content: Local authorities are now enforcing this contravention using CCTV enforcement. Quite often however the signs are not sufficiently clear. However some like Camden in London are continually criticised for not installing compliant signage. They are quick to enforce but often ignore adjudicators decisions against them..
sections.contentHtml: Local authorities are now enforcing this contravention using CCTV enforcement. Quite often however the signs are not sufficiently clear. However some like Camden in London are continually criticised for not installing compliant signage. They are quick to enforce but often ignore adjudicators decisions against them..

sections.heading: Sample signs:
How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
2 Check: What a Penalty Charge Notice must contain
3 Check: Statutory grounds for appeal for Moving Traffic Contraventions
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)

2 Check: What a Penalty Charge Notice must contain

3 Check: Statutory grounds for appeal for Moving Traffic Contraventions

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
As the contravention is that of “failing to comply with a sign indicating restrictions on entering a pedestrian zone, (or entering and waiting in a pedestrian zone)” it is important to remember that in order to prove this contravention that the enforcing authority must actually show you passing the sign during restricted hours and subsequent movement by the vehicle including whether it parks, stops or traverses the restricted length/area It is not sufficient to simply show you in or entering a pedestrian zone. You can ask to see the video or photographic evidence. This only applies if the contravention includes the words “failing to comply with a sign” if it does not then they must in any event have clear signage in order to be able to enforce the contravention.
There are 5 more ways that you can have your penalty charge notice cancelled for this offence. We have even summarised adjudicator’s decisions where motorists have won.
Go to top
 
Was the signage clear, illuminated and placed before the restrictions actually start.
Were there road markings, if so did they contain exactly the same restrictions as the signs.
Were you loading/unloading? Usually there will be an exemption relating to loading to premises within the zone. If you were loading within the street, or in fact drove through the street but parked elsewhere in order to return to the street to make a delivery, then you may not have committed a contravention.
Were you displaying a disabled badge as quite often there are concessions relating to disabled badge holders.
If there are restrictions at certain times were times stated on the Penalty charge notice (PCN) actually the same as on the signs.
Also were there signs warning of CCTV camera enforcement in the vicinity. Whilst these are not compulsory under the traffic management act they are compulsory under the guidlines issued by the information commissioner’s office (ICO)There is no guarantee however that an adjudicator would rule in your favour as we had a case where he did not. although in another case the adjudicator did!
Finally remember that you may cite compassionate grounds in an appeal against a Penalty Charge Notice.
 
Now take a look at the adjudicators decisions for this contravention.
 
Go to top
 
Adjudicators decisions for the contravention of failing to comply with a sign indicating restrictions on vehicles entering a pedestrian zone
 
CASE NO: 206 0511321 – Camden Council
Laystall Street
The Appeal was allowed because “the video evidence supplied by the Council shows the Appellant’s vehicle leaving a junction near some railings and a ‘No Entry’ sign where this is in relation to the signage relied on and how it proves the contravention alleged is far from clear.  I cannot be satisfied that the contravention took place”.  The Appeal was allowed.  In this location, Camden continues to enforce the restrictions despite many adjudicators’ decisions saying they are not correctly signed.
Back to Moving Traffic

Also were there signs warning of CCTV camera enforcement in the vicinity. Whilst these are not compulsory under the traffic management act they are compulsory under the guidlines issued by the information commissioner’s office (ICO)There is no guarantee however that an adjudicator would rule in your favour as we had a case where he did not. although in another case the adjudicator did!

Finally remember that you may cite compassionate grounds in an appeal against a Penalty Charge Notice.

Adjudicators decisions for the contravention of failing to comply with a sign indicating restrictions on vehicles entering a pedestrian zone

 

CASE NO: 206 0511321 – Camden Council
Laystall Street

The Appeal was allowed because “the video evidence supplied by the Council shows the Appellant’s vehicle leaving a junction near some railings and a ‘No Entry’ sign where this is in relation to the signage relied on and how it proves the contravention alleged is far from clear.  I cannot be satisfied that the contravention took place”.  The Appeal was allowed.  In this location, Camden continues to enforce the restrictions despite many adjudicators’ decisions saying they are not correctly signed.
